source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set
input_row.description: Do	not	use	token	based	authentication.
input_row.rationale: The	token-based	authentication	utilizes	static	tokens	to	authenticate	requests	to	the apiserver.	The	tokens	are	stored	in	clear-text	in	a	file	on	the	apiserver,	and	cannot	be revoked	or	rotated	without	restarting	the	apiserver.	Hence,	do	not	use	static	token-based authentication. Impact: You	will	have	to	configure	and	use	alternate	authentication	mechanisms	such	as certificates.	Static	token	based	authentication	could	not	be	used.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--token-auth-file	argument	does	not	exist.
input_row.remediation: Follow	the	documentation	and	configure	alternate	mechanisms	for	authentication.	Then, edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	remove	the	--token-auth-file=<filename> parameter. Default	Value: By	default,	--token-auth-file	argument	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authentication/#static-token-file 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 60	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 16.14	Encrypt/Hash	All	Authentication	Files	And	Monitor	Their	Access Verify	that	all	authentication	files	are	encrypted	or	hashed	and	that	these	files	cannot	be accessed	without	root	or	administrator	privileges.	Audit	all	access	to	password	files	in	the system. Version	7 16.4	Encrypt	or	Hash	all	Authentication	Credentials Encrypt	or	hash	with	a	salt	all	authentication	credentials	when	stored. 61	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: You	will	have	to	configure	and	use	alternate	authentication	mechanisms	such	as certificates.	Static	token	based	authentication	could	not	be	used.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authentication/#static-token-file 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 60	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 16.14	Encrypt/Hash	All	Authentication	Files	And	Monitor	Their	Access Verify	that	all	authentication	files	are	encrypted	or	hashed	and	that	these	files	cannot	be accessed	without	root	or	administrator	privileges.	Audit	all	access	to	password	files	in	the system. Version	7 16.4	Encrypt	or	Hash	all	Authentication	Credentials Encrypt	or	hash	with	a	salt	all	authentication	credentials	when	stored. 61	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set" requires checking the configuration of the Kubernetes API server on the master node to ensure that the `--token-auth-file` argument does not exist in the process arguments.

1. The audit field suggests using a command (`ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver`) on the master node, which indicates the need for SSH access to the node to inspect runtime parameters directly.
2. The impact and remediation fields indicate the modification of the API server pod specification, which is typically a manual operation involving SSH access to the server and editing configuration files.
3. References and additional information suggest that this control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters through standard APIs, reinforcing the need for manual examination.

Given these points, the audit requires manual inspection and cannot be automated via Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI tools. It involves a hands-on review of the configuration files/arguments on the Kubernetes master node.

**MANUAL STEPS:** 
1. **SSH into the master node**: Use an SSH client to log into the Kubernetes master node of your ACK cluster.
2. **Inspect API server process**: Run `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the current running arguments of the Kubernetes API server.
3. **Verify absence of --token-auth-file**: Carefully check the output to ensure that there is no `--token-auth-file=<filename>` parameter present in the command-line arguments of any `kube-apiserver` process.
4. **Compliance confirmation**: The master node is compliant if the `--token-auth-file` parameter is not present in the current arguments of the API server. If present, follow remediation steps to remove it and ensure alternative authentication mechanisms are configured.
